Question: Define a point.
Answer: A point is an exact position in space with no length

Question: Define a line.
Answer: A line is a straight path of points that extends infinitely in both directions.

Question: Define a line segment.
Answer: A line segment is a part of a line with two endpoints.

Question: Define a ray.
Answer: A ray is a part of a line with one endpoint that extends infinitely in one direction.

Question: Define a plane.
Answer: A plane is a flat surface that extends infinitely in two dimensions.

Question: Define a polygon.
Answer: A polygon is a closed figure formed by three or more line segments.

Question: Define a triangle.
Answer: A triangle is a polygon with three sides.

Question: Define a quadrilateral.
Answer: A quadrilateral is a polygon with four sides.

Question: Define a pentagon.
Answer: A pentagon is a polygon with five sides.

Question: Define a hexagon.
Answer: A hexagon is a polygon with six sides.

Question: Define a heptagon.
Answer: A heptagon is a polygon with seven sides.

Question: Define an octagon.
Answer: An octagon is a polygon with eight sides.

Question: Define a circle.
Answer: A circle is a closed curved figure where all points are equidistant from the centre.

Question: Define radius of a circle.
Answer: A radius is a line segment from the centre of a circle to any point on the circumference.

Question: Define diameter of a circle.
Answer: A diameter is a line passing through the centre of a circle and touches two points on the circumference.

Question: Define circumference.
Answer: Circumference is the distance around the circle.

Question: Define tangent.
Answer: A tangent is a line that touches the circle at exactly one point.

Question: Define vertex.
Answer: A vertex is the corner point of a polygon where two sides meet.

Question: Define diagonal.
Answer: A diagonal is a line joining any two non-adjacent vertices of a polygon.

Question: Define angle.
Answer: An angle is formed when two rays meet at a common endpoint.

Question: Define acute angle.
Answer: An acute angle measures less than 90 degrees.

Question: Define right angle.
Answer: A right angle measures exactly 90 degrees.

Question: Define obtuse angle.
Answer: An obtuse angle measures more than 90 degrees but less than 180 degrees.

Question: Define straight angle.
Answer: A straight angle measures exactly 180 degrees.

Question: Define full rotation.
Answer: A full rotation is an angle of 360 degrees.

Question: Define perimeter.
Answer: Perimeter is the total distance around a closed figure.

Question: Define area.
Answer: Area is the space enclosed by a closed figure.

Question: Define equilateral triangle.
Answer: A triangle with all sides equal and all angles equal.

Question: Define isosceles triangle.
Answer: A triangle with two sides equal and two angles equal.

Question: Define scalene triangle.
Answer: A triangle with all sides unequal and all angles unequal.

Question: Define right-angled triangle.
Answer: A triangle with one right angle.

Question: Define acute triangle.
Answer: A triangle in which all angles are less than 90 degrees.

Question: Define obtuse triangle.
Answer: A triangle in which one angle is greater than 90 degrees.

Question: Define rectangle.
Answer: A quadrilateral with four right angles and opposite sides equal.

Question: Define square.
Answer: A quadrilateral with four right angles and all sides equal.

Question: Define composite figure.
Answer: A figure made by combining two or more simple geometric shapes.

Question: Define chord of a circle.
Answer: A chord is a line segment joining two points on the circumference of a circle.

Question: Define arc of a circle.
Answer: An arc is a part of the circumference of a circle.

Question: Define secant of a circle.
Answer: A secant is a line that intersects a circle at two points.

Question: Explain the difference between radius and diameter.
Answer: Radius is half of diameter; diameter passes through the centre and is twice the radius.

Question: Explain the difference between a polygon and a circle.
Answer: A polygon has straight sides; a circle has a curved boundary.

Question: Define congruent shapes.
Answer: Congruent shapes are shapes that are identical in shape and size.

Question: Define similar shapes.
Answer: Similar shapes have the same shape but not necessarily the same size.
